snp4.5:1.2: a person makes them the utmost thing in the world,
snp4.5:1.3: &, from that, calls all others inferior
snp4.5:1.4: and so he’s not gone beyond disputes.
snp4.5:2.1: When he sees his own advantage
snp4.5:2.2: in what’s seen, heard, sensed, or in habits & practices,
snp4.5:2.4: he sees all else, all others, as inferior.
snp4.5:3.1: That, too, say the skilled, is a binding knot:
snp4.5:3.2: that in dependence on which you regard another as inferior.
snp4.5:3.3: So a monk shouldn’t be dependent on what’s seen, heard, or sensed,
snp4.5:4.1: nor should he theorize a view in the world
snp4.5:4.2: in connection with knowledge or habits & practices;
snp4.5:4.4: shouldn’t think himself inferior or superlative.
snp4.5:5.1: Abandoning what he’d embraced, not clinging,
snp4.5:5.2: he doesn’t make himself dependent even in connection with knowledge;
snp4.5:5.3: doesn’t follow a faction among those who are split;
snp4.5:5.4: doesn’t fall back on any view whatsoever.
snp4.5:6.1: One who isn’t inclined toward either side
snp4.5:6.4: when considering what’s grasped among doctrines,
snp4.5:7.2: with regard to what’s seen, heard, or sensed.
snp4.5:7.3: By whom, with what, should he be pigeonholed here in the world?
snp4.5:8.3: A brahman not led by habits or practices,
snp4.5:8.4: gone to the beyond —Such— doesn’t fall back.
